rules.structural_signal: user input concatenated into query string
rules.rationale: String concatenation in queries is the most common injection vector

rules.structural_signal: user input passed to os.system or subprocess with shell=True
rules.rationale: Shell execution with user input enables arbitrary command execution

rules.structural_signal: password stored with MD5 or SHA1
rules.rationale: Weak hashing algorithms can be cracked in minutes with modern hardware

rules.structural_signal: JWT decoded without signature verification
rules.rationale: Unverified JWTs allow token forgery and authentication bypass

rules.structural_signal: database ID from URL used directly without ownership check
rules.rationale: Direct object references without authorization enable horizontal privilege escalation

rules.structural_signal: user input rendered in HTML without escaping
rules.rationale: Unescaped user content in HTML enables cross-site scripting attacks

rules.structural_signal: API key hardcoded in source code
rules.rationale: Hardcoded secrets are exposed through version control and decompilation

rules.structural_signal: debug mode enabled in production configuration
rules.rationale: Debug mode exposes interactive debuggers and source code in production

rules.structural_signal: no rate limiting on authentication endpoint
rules.rationale: Unlimited login attempts enable credential stuffing and brute force attacks

rules.structural_signal: user-supplied URL fetched by server without validation
rules.rationale: Unvalidated server-side URL fetching enables SSRF to internal services

rules.structural_signal: CORS origin set to wildcard with credentials
rules.rationale: Permissive CORS with credentials allows cross-origin data theft

rules.structural_signal: XML parser does not disable external entity processing
rules.rationale: XXE enables file reading, SSRF, and denial of service via entity expansion

rules.structural_signal: pickle.loads used on user-supplied data
rules.rationale: Pickle deserialization of untrusted data enables remote code execution

rules.structural_signal: user input used in file path without sanitization
rules.rationale: Unsanitized file paths allow reading arbitrary files via ../ traversal

rules.structural_signal: session ID not regenerated after authentication
rules.rationale: Static session IDs after login enable session fixation attacks

rules.structural_signal: SSL certificate verification disabled
rules.rationale: Disabled certificate verification enables man-in-the-middle attacks

rules.structural_signal: random module used for security token generation
rules.rationale: Python random module is predictable and unsuitable for security tokens

rules.structural_signal: request body directly assigned to model object
rules.rationale: Mass assignment allows attackers to modify protected fields like role or is_admin

rules.structural_signal: admin endpoint without authentication decorator
rules.rationale: Unprotected admin endpoints allow unauthorized access to sensitive functions

rules.structural_signal: user input passed directly to template render
rules.rationale: Template injection enables remote code execution on the server

rules.structural_signal: LLM output passed to eval or exec
rules.rationale: Executing LLM output as code bridges prompt injection to code execution

rules.structural_signal: agent tool calls not validated against allowlist
rules.rationale: Unrestricted tool access enables privilege escalation and data exfiltration

rules.structural_signal: user input contains instruction override patterns
rules.rationale: Instruction overrides in user input lead to complete agent takeover

rules.structural_signal: retrieved content contains hidden instructions
rules.rationale: Indirect injection through data sources is harder to detect than direct injection

rules.structural_signal: agent makes HTTP request to unexpected external domain
rules.rationale: Unrestricted external requests enable data exfiltration via agent tools

rules.structural_signal: MCP server connected from unverified source
rules.rationale: Malicious MCP servers can poison tools, context, and exfiltrate data

rules.structural_signal: agent writes user input directly to persistent memory
rules.rationale: Unsanitized memory writes create persistent backdoors across sessions

rules.structural_signal: file uploaded without content type validation
rules.rationale: Unvalidated file uploads can deliver web shells and malware

rules.structural_signal: API response returns full database object without field filtering
rules.rationale: Returning raw database objects exposes internal fields and sensitive data

rules.structural_signal: dependencies not pinned to specific versions
rules.rationale: Unpinned dependencies may silently pull in vulnerable or compromised versions

rules.structural_signal: HTTP connection used instead of HTTPS for external API
rules.rationale: Cleartext HTTP enables interception of sensitive data in transit

rules.structural_signal: security headers not set on HTTP responses
rules.rationale: Missing security headers leave browsers vulnerable to clickjacking and XSS

rules.structural_signal: user input logged without sanitization
rules.rationale: Unsanitized log input enables log forging and log injection attacks

rules.structural_signal: GraphQL introspection enabled in production
rules.rationale: GraphQL introspection reveals entire API schema to attackers

rules.structural_signal: password comparison using equality operator
rules.rationale: Equality comparison leaks password information through timing side channels

rules.structural_signal: redirect URL taken from query parameter without validation
rules.rationale: Open redirects enable phishing attacks using the trusted domain

rules.structural_signal: user role updatable via client request
rules.rationale: Client-controlled roles enable trivial privilege escalation to admin

rules.structural_signal: default admin credentials in configuration
rules.rationale: Default credentials are publicly known and enable immediate unauthorized access

rules.structural_signal: sensitive data stored in shared cache without access control
rules.rationale: Unprotected cached data accessible to any user sharing the cache

rules.structural_signal: LDAP filter built with string formatting from user input
rules.rationale: LDAP filter injection can bypass authentication and extract directory data

rules.structural_signal: no token budget or step limit on agent execution
rules.rationale: Unbounded agent execution enables cost explosion and service denial

rules.structural_signal: error response includes stack trace or system prompt
rules.rationale: Information disclosure aids attacker reconnaissance for further exploits

rules.structural_signal: fine-tuning data loaded without validation or provenance check
rules.rationale: Contaminated training data can introduce persistent backdoors in models

rules.structural_signal: agent framework dependencies not pinned with integrity hashes
rules.rationale: Unpinned agent dependencies risk pulling in compromised library versions

rules.structural_signal: nested quantifiers in regex processing user input
rules.rationale: Nested quantifiers cause catastrophic backtracking on crafted inputs

rules.structural_signal: encryption key stored in source code or configuration
rules.rationale: Keys in code or config are exposed through version control and backups

rules.structural_signal: CI pipeline runs untrusted code from pull requests
rules.rationale: Untrusted PR code in CI can steal secrets and compromise build artifacts

rules.structural_signal: admin panel accessible without VPN or IP restriction
rules.rationale: Internet-exposed admin panels are prime targets for brute force and exploitation

rules.structural_signal: PII fields included in log messages
rules.rationale: PII in logs violates privacy regulations and creates breach liability

rules.structural_signal: innerHTML set from user-controlled data
rules.rationale: innerHTML with user data enables DOM-based cross-site scripting

rules.structural_signal: database backup stored in web-accessible directory
rules.rationale: Web-accessible database dumps expose entire datasets to public download

rules.structural_signal: input requests agent to adopt unrestricted persona
rules.rationale: Persona manipulation gradually erodes safety guardrails across turns

rules.structural_signal: user input concatenated into LDAP filter or XPath expression
rules.rationale: Unescaped input in LDAP/XPath enables query manipulation and data extraction

rules.structural_signal: MFA check only on frontend not enforced server-side
rules.rationale: Client-side-only MFA is trivially bypassed by directly calling the API

rules.structural_signal: MongoDB query built directly from request JSON body
rules.rationale: NoSQL operator injection in MongoDB bypasses authentication and extracts data
